assertions.title: RecoveryRestoreScreen — accepts 12- or 24-word mnemonic input
assertions.reason: The focused recovery-restore Jest suite passed and explicitly covered the disabled CTA before a complete phrase, 24-word normalization, and 12-word normalization, with restoreFromMnemonic called exactly once with the canonical lower-cased single-spaced mnemonic.

commands: rg -n -e '^\s*it\(' -e 'disables the "Restore wallet" CTA before a valid mnemonic has been typed' -e 'calls restoreFromMnemonic exactly once with the normalized 24-word mnemonic on submit' -e 'also supports a 12-word mnemonic' src/features/auth/screens/recovery-restore-screen.test.tsx
testNames: disables the "Restore wallet" CTA before a valid mnemonic has been typed
calls restoreFromMnemonic exactly once with the normalized 24-word mnemonic on submit

assertions.title: RecoveryRestoreScreen — valid mnemonic restores vault + routes to Main
assertions.reason: The success-path tests passed: after restore the session snapshot is atomically hydrated back to the Main-eligible state (`biometricStatus: 'ready'`, `hasCompletedOnboarding: true`, `hasIdentity: true`, `isLocked: false`) and the screen fires `onRestored` exactly once after awaiting `hydrateRestored()`, which is the navigator handoff for the declarative route back to Main.

commands: rg -n -e 'sets session.biometricStatus to "ready" and hydrates identity flags on successful restore' -e 'awaits hydrateRestored before calling onRestored \(cold-kill durability race fix\)' -e 'commits the restored session exactly once via useSessionStore.hydrateRestored\(\)' src/features/auth/screens/recovery-restore-screen.test.tsx
testNames: sets session.biometricStatus to "ready" and hydrates identity flags on successful restore
awaits hydrateRestored before calling onRestored (cold-kill durability race fix)
commits the restored session exactly once via useSessionStore.hydrateRestored()

assertions.title: RecoveryRestoreScreen — invalid mnemonic surfaces error
assertions.reason: The invalid-mnemonic path passed: an invalid BIP-39 phrase never reaches restoreFromMnemonic, an inline `role="alert"` error remains visible with user-readable recovery-phrase copy, and the typed input value is retained for retry.

commands: rg -n -e 'shows a clear role="alert" message on an invalid mnemonic and does NOT call restore' -e 'rejects a short mnemonic with an inline alert and never calls restore' src/features/auth/screens/recovery-restore-screen.test.tsx
testNames: shows a clear role="alert" message on an invalid mnemonic and does NOT call restore
rejects a short mnemonic with an inline alert and never calls restore

assertions.title: RecoveryRestoreScreen — re-arms biometric setup after restore
assertions.reason: The biometric resealing coverage passed: the successful-restore test drives the restore mock through `NativeBiometricVault.generateAndStoreSecret(...)` and asserts exactly one reseal plus `biometricStatus === 'ready'`, confirming the restore flow re-arms biometric protection instead of only hydrating JS state.

commands: rg -n -e 're-arms biometric protection exactly once after a successful restore' src/features/auth/screens/recovery-restore-screen.test.tsx
testNames: re-arms biometric protection exactly once after a successful restore

commandsRun.observation: PASS; the focused recovery-restore suite passed all 17 tests.

commandsRun.observation: PASS; the test-file scan located the exact Jest cases backing normalization, success-path hydration, invalid-phrase error handling, and biometric resealing coverage.
PASS; the implementation scan captured the mnemonic normalization helper, BIP-39 validation gate, restore call, durable session hydration, navigator handoff, and inline alert rendering points.

summary: Tested 4 assigned assertions on the jest-local surface: 4 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. The focused recovery-restore suite passed all 17 tests, and the saved evidence logs map normalized input handling, success-path session hydration, invalid-phrase errors, and biometric resealing to explicit test and implementation lines.
